17	// newWorkCommand creates the top-level "ddx work" command: the FEAT-010 layer-3

22	func (f *CommandFactory) newWorkCommand() *cobra.Command {
23		cmd := &cobra.Command{
24			Use:   "work",
25			Short: "Drain the bead execution queue",
26			Long: `work drains the execution-ready bead queue. It is the FEAT-010 layer-3
27	queue drain: it iterates ddx try (layer 2) across ready beads until a stop
28	condition is met and owns retry-power policy between attempts.
29	
30	ddx work treats --harness, --provider, --model, and --profile as opaque
31	passthrough constraints forwarded to Fizeau unchanged. DDx does not validate
32	these values or branch on them; Fizeau owns routing within the requested power
33	bounds.
34	
35	Review is on by default. --no-review is a break-glass override and
36	requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing. A bead label of review:skip
37	is only honored when it also carries a sibling review:skip-reason:*
38	label; otherwise the label is ignored.
39	
40	Stop conditions (evaluated between attempts):
41	  drained     — no ready beads remain
42	  blocked     — every remaining bead has produced a terminal non-success outcome
43	  deferred    — configured budget exhausted
44	  no_progress — N consecutive attempts produced no commit (default N=3)
45	  signal      — SIGINT/SIGTERM received between attempts
46	
47	work runs inline in the current process; per ADR-022 there is no separate
48	"submit to server" mode. The legacy --local flag is accepted but ignored
49	(deprecation warning printed) and will be removed in a future release.
50	`,
51			Example: `  # Drain the current execution-ready queue and exit
52	  ddx work
53	
54	  # Pick one ready bead, execute it, and stop
55	  ddx work --once
56	
57	  # Watch for newly-ready work after the current queue drains
58	  ddx work --watch
59	
60	  # Watch with a shorter idle scan interval
61	  ddx work --watch --idle-interval 15s
62	
63	  # Forward harness/model as passthrough constraints (ddx does not validate these)
64	  ddx work --once --harness agent --model minimax/minimax-m2.7
65	
66	  # Skip review only with the break-glass acknowledgement flag
67	  ddx work --once --no-review --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing
68	
69	  # Constrain power powerClass (retry-power policy is owned by ddx work)
70	  ddx work --once --min-power 40 --max-power 90`,
71			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
72			RunE: f.runWork,

127	// runWork is the RunE for "ddx work". It drives the FEAT-010 layer-3 queue
128	// drain with harness/provider/model treated as opaque passthrough constraints —
129	// ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService is not called; DDx does not inspect or
130	// branch on those values. Retry-power policy (min-power / max-power) is owned
131	// by this layer.
132	func (f *CommandFactory) runWork(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
133		if err := f.checkLifecycleMigrationGate(cmd); err != nil {
134			return err
135		}
136	
137		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
138		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
139		lockmetrics.SetSink(lockmetrics.FileSink(projectRoot))
140		lockmetrics.SetCapEnforcement(projectRoot, "")
141		f.warnIfInstalledBinaryBehindSource(cmd)
142	
143		// Preflight: warn once per process for degraded project-local skill layout.
144		// Suppressed in JSON mode to avoid corrupting machine-readable output
145		// (stderr and stdout are merged by some test helpers and log aggregators).
146		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
147		if !asJSON {
148			preflightResult := checkProjectRuntimePreflight(projectRoot)
149			f.preflightWarnOnce.Do(func() {
150				emitPreflightWarning(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), preflightResult)
151			})
152		}
153	
154		return f.runAgentExecuteLoopImpl(cmd, true, "")
155	}

157	func writeExecuteLoopResult(w io.Writer, projectRoot string, result *agent.ExecuteBeadLoopResult, asJSON bool) error {
158		if asJSON {
159			payload := struct {
160				ProjectRoot string `json:"project_root"`
161				*agent.ExecuteBeadLoopResult
162			}{
163				ProjectRoot:           projectRoot,
164				ExecuteBeadLoopResult: result,
165			}
166			enc := json.NewEncoder(w)
167			enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
168			return enc.Encode(payload)
169		}
170	
171		if result.NoReadyWork && result.Attempts == 0 {
172			writeNoReadyWorkSummary(w, projectRoot, result.NoReadyWorkDetail)
173			writeQueueSnapshotTerminalSummary(w, result.QueueSnapshot)
174			return nil
175		}
176	
177		fmt.Fprintf(w, "\nproject: %s\n", projectRoot)
178		writeWorkTerminalSummary(w, result)
179		writeOperatorAttentionSummary(w, result.OperatorAttention)
180		if result.Failures > 0 {
181			fmt.Fprintf(w, "\nfailed:\n")
182			for _, attempt := range result.Results {
183				if attempt.Status != "success" {
184					fmt.Fprintf(w, "  - %s: %s", attempt.BeadID, attempt.Detail)
185					if attempt.PreserveRef != "" {
186						fmt.Fprintf(w, " (preserved)")
187					}
188					fmt.Fprintln(w)
189				}
190			}
191		}
192		if result.NoReadyWork {
193			fmt.Fprintln(w)
194			writeNoReadyWorkSummary(w, "", result.NoReadyWorkDetail)
195			writeQueueSnapshotTerminalSummary(w, result.QueueSnapshot)
196		}
197		return nil
198	}

200	func writeWorkTerminalSummary(w io.Writer, result *agent.ExecuteBeadLoopResult) {
201		closed, changed, alreadySatisfied := countWorkTerminalOutcomes(result)
202		fmt.Fprintf(w, "worker exited: %s\n", workExitSummary(result))
203		fmt.Fprintf(w, "attempts: %d  |  closed: %d  |  changed: %d  |  already-satisfied: %d  |  failures: %d\n",
204			result.Attempts, closed, changed, alreadySatisfied, result.Failures)
205	}

207	func writeOperatorAttentionSummary(w io.Writer, stop *agent.OperatorAttentionStop) {
208		if stop == nil {
209			return
210		}
211		fmt.Fprintln(w)
212		fmt.Fprintln(w, "operator attention:")
213		if stop.BeadID != "" {
214			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  released bead: %s\n", stop.BeadID)
215		}
216		if stop.Message != "" {
217			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  %s\n", stop.Message)
218		}
219		if stop.ProjectRoot != "" {
220			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  project root: %s\n", stop.ProjectRoot)
221		}
222		if len(stop.DirtyPaths) > 0 {
223			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  dirty paths: %s\n", strings.Join(stop.DirtyPaths, ", "))
224		}
225	}
226	
227	func countWorkTerminalOutcomes(result *agent.ExecuteBeadLoopResult) (closed, changed, alreadySatisfied int) {
228		for _, attempt := range result.Results {
229			switch attempt.Status {
230			case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess:
231				closed++
232				changed++
233			case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied:
234				closed++
235				alreadySatisfied++
236			}
237		}
238		if closed == 0 && result.Successes > 0 {
239			closed = result.Successes
240		}
241		return closed, changed, alreadySatisfied
242	}
243	
244	func workExitSummary(result *agent.ExecuteBeadLoopResult) string {
245		switch result.ExitReason {
246		case "drained":
247			return "drained current execution-ready queue"
248		case "once_complete":
249			return "once complete"
250		case "budget":
251			return "budget reached"
252		case "no_progress":
253			return "no-progress policy stopped work"
254		case "blocked":
255			return "blocked waiting for external action"
256		case "operator_attention":
257			return "operator attention required"
258		case "sigint", "sigterm", "context_cancelled":
259			return "stopped by signal"
260		case "fatal_config":
261			return "fatal configuration error"
262		case "preflight_failed":
263			return "preflight failed"
264		case "resource_exhausted":
265			return "resource exhausted"
266		case "routing_unavailable":
267			return "routing unavailable"
268		}
269		if result.StopCondition != "" {
270			return strings.ToLower(strings.ReplaceAll(result.StopCondition, "_", " "))
271		}
272		return "completed"
273	}

305	func writeNoReadyWorkSummary(w io.Writer, projectRoot string, d agent.NoReadyWorkBreakdown) {
306		if projectRoot != "" {
307			fmt.Fprintf(w, "project: %s\n", projectRoot)

55	func TestLoop_StaysAliveWithEmptyQueue(t *testing.T) {

68		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
69

71		// wall-clock for several empty-poll cycles to confirm the loop is still
72		// running and not bailing out after the first empty poll.
73		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
74		pollInterval := 5 * time.Millisecond
75		cancelAfter := 50 * time.Millisecond
76		go func() {

92		assert.Equal(t, 0, result.Attempts)

311	// TestWorkWatch_PreClaimIdleEscalatesAfterRepeatedSameDetail covers
312	// ddx-df77e668 AC #3: when the worker idles on the same pre-claim blocker for
313	// preClaimIdleEscalationThreshold consecutive cycles, it emits a non-terminal
314	// loop.operator_attention event carrying the bead-id, detail, and elapsed-idle
315	// instead of looping silently. The beads are never cooldowned, and the loop
316	// keeps idling (the escalation does not stop the worker).
317	func TestWorkWatch_PreClaimIdleEscalatesAfterRepeatedSameDetail(t *testing.T) {
318		store, first, second := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
319		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
320			Store: store,
321			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
322				t.Errorf("executor must not run while pre-claim idles")
323				return ExecuteBeadReport{}, nil
324			}),
325		}
326	
327		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
328		defer cancel()
329		progressCh := make(chan ProgressEvent, 256)
330		done := make(chan struct{})
331		var idleCount int32
332		go func() {
333			for {
334				select {
335				case evt := <-progressCh:
336					if evt.Phase == "loop.idle" && evt.Message == preClaimIdleReasonTrackerContention {
337						if atomic.AddInt32(&idleCount, 1) >= int32(preClaimIdleEscalationThreshold)+2 {
338							cancel()
339							return
340						}
341					}
342				case <-done:
343					return
344				}
345			}
346		}()
347		defer close(done)
348	
349		var eventSink, logBuf bytes.Buffer
350		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
351		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
352		result, err := worker.Run(ctx, rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
353			Mode:         executeloop.ModeWatch,
354			IdleInterval: time.Millisecond,
355			Log:          &logBuf,
356			EventSink:    &eventSink,
357			ProgressCh:   progressCh,
358			SessionID:    "sess-escalate",
359			WorkerID:     "worker-escalate",
360			PreClaimHook: func(ctx context.Context) error {
361				return errors.New("landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s:\nM\t.ddx/beads.jsonl\nM\t.ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl")
362			},
363		})
364	
365		require.ErrorIs(t, err, context.Canceled)
366		require.NotNil(t, result)
367		assert.Equal(t, 0, result.Attempts)
368		assert.Equal(t, 0, result.Failures)
369		// The escalation is non-terminal: it does not populate a terminal stop.
370		assert.Nil(t, result.OperatorAttention)
371	
372		// Beads stay open and uncooldowned — tracker contention never faults a bead.
373		gotFirst, err := store.Get(first.ID)
374		require.NoError(t, err)
375		gotSecond, err := store.Get(second.ID)
376		require.NoError(t, err)
377		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, gotFirst.Status)
378		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, gotSecond.Status)
379		assert.Nil(t, gotFirst.Extra["work-retry-after"])
380	
381		// Exactly one operator-attention escalation event is emitted for the streak.
382		lines := strings.Split(strings.TrimSpace(eventSink.String()), "\n")
383		var escalations []map[string]any
384		for _, line := range lines {
385			if strings.TrimSpace(line) == "" {
386				continue
387			}
388			var entry map[string]any
389			require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &entry))
390			if entry["type"] != "loop.operator_attention" {
391				continue
392			}
393			data, ok := entry["data"].(map[string]any)
394			require.True(t, ok)
395			if data["reason"] == "preclaim_idle_escalation" {
396				escalations = append(escalations, data)
397			}
398		}
399		require.Len(t, escalations, 1, "exactly one escalation event per same-detail streak")
400		esc := escalations[0]
401		assert.Contains(t, []any{first.ID, second.ID}, esc["bead_id"], "escalation must name a skipped bead")
402		assert.Contains(t, esc["detail"], ".ddx/beads.jsonl", "escalation must carry the blocker detail")
403		elapsed, ok := esc["elapsed_idle"].(string)
404		require.True(t, ok, "escalation must carry elapsed_idle")
405		assert.NotEmpty(t, elapsed)
406		idleCountVal, ok := esc["idle_count"].(float64)
407		require.True(t, ok)
408		assert.GreaterOrEqual(t, int(idleCountVal), preClaimIdleEscalationThreshold)
409	
410		assert.Contains(t, logBuf.String(), "operator attention: pre-claim idled")
411	}

432		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
433			Store: workerStore,
434			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
435				return ExecuteBeadReport{

447			Mode:                      executeloop.ModeDrain,

449			EventSink:                 &eventSink,
450			WorkerID:                  "worker-claim-rate",
451			SessionID:                 "sess-claim-rate",
452			ClaimSuccessRateWindow:    3,
453			ClaimSuccessRateThreshold: 0.5,
454		})
455	
456		require.ErrorIs(t, err, context.Canceled)
457		require.NotNil(t, result)
458		assert.Equal(t, 2, strings.Count(logBuf.String(), "claim success rate"), "warning should emit once per below-threshold crossing")
459	
460		lines := strings.Split(strings.TrimSpace(eventSink.String()), "\n")
461		var warnings []map[string]any
462		for _, line := range lines {
463			if strings.TrimSpace(line) == "" {
464				continue

466			var entry map[string]any
467			require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &entry))
468			if entry["type"] != "loop.operator_attention" {

471			data, ok := entry["data"].(map[string]any)
472			require.True(t, ok)

474				continue
475			}
476			warnings = append(warnings, data)
477		}
478	
479		require.Len(t, warnings, 2, "a later success must clear the warned state so a new crossing warns again")
480		assert.Equal(t, float64(3), warnings[0]["window_size"])
481		assert.Equal(t, float64(0.5), warnings[0]["threshold"])
482		assert.Equal(t, float64(0), warnings[0]["successes"])
483		assert.Equal(t, float64(3), warnings[0]["non_successes"])
484		assert.Equal(t, float64(3), warnings[1]["window_size"])
485		assert.Equal(t, float64(0.5), warnings[1]["threshold"])
486		assert.Equal(t, float64(1), warnings[1]["successes"])
487		assert.Equal(t, float64(2), warnings[1]["non_successes"])
488	}

1055	func TestWorkWatchStdout_PrintsNextReadyTransitionAfterIdle(t *testing.T) {
1056		store := bead.NewStore(t.TempDir())
1057		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
1058	
1059		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
1060		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
1061			Store: store,
1062			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
1063				cancel()
1064				return ExecuteBeadReport{
1065					BeadID:    beadID,
1066					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
1067					SessionID: "sess-after-idle",
1068					ResultRev: "feedface",
1069				}, nil
1070			}),
1071			Now: fixedWatchStdoutTime,
1072		}
1073	
1074		wakeCh := make(chan struct{}, 1)
1075		progressCh := make(chan ProgressEvent, 16)
1076	
1077		var logBuf bytes.Buffer
1078		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
1079		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
1080	
1081		// Run the worker in a goroutine so the main goroutine can process progress
1082		// events synchronously, eliminating goroutine-scheduling races under load.
1083		type workerResult struct {
1084			result *ExecuteBeadLoopResult
1085			err    error
1086		}
1087		workerDone := make(chan workerResult, 1)
1088		go func() {
1089			r, e := worker.Run(ctx, rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
1090				Mode:         executeloop.ModeWatch,
1091				IdleInterval: time.Hour,
1092				Log:          &logBuf,
1093				ProgressCh:   progressCh,
1094				WakeCh:       wakeCh,
1095			})
1096			workerDone <- workerResult{r, e}
1097		}()
1098	
1099		// Deterministic: on loop.idle, create the bead and send the wake signal
1100		// immediately from the main goroutine with no scheduling gap.
1101		var activeEvents int32
1102		var beadCreateErr error
1103		var wr workerResult
1104		idleHandled := false
1105		finished := false
1106		for !finished {
1107			select {
1108			case evt := <-progressCh:
1109				switch evt.Phase {
1110				case "loop.idle":
1111					if !idleHandled {
1112						idleHandled = true
1113						beadCreateErr = store.Create(&bead.Bead{
1114							ID:       "ddx-ready-after-idle",
1115							Title:    "spec: define full DDx temp cleanup in work cycle",
1116							Priority: 0,
1117						})
1118						wakeCh <- struct{}{}
1119					}
1120				case "loop.active":
1121					atomic.AddInt32(&activeEvents, 1)
1122				}
1123			case wr = <-workerDone:
1124				finished = true
1125			}
1126		}
1127		// Drain events emitted just before the worker signalled done.
1128	drainLoop:
1129		for {
1130			select {
1131			case evt := <-progressCh:
1132				if evt.Phase == "loop.active" {
1133					atomic.AddInt32(&activeEvents, 1)
1134				}
1135			default:
1136				break drainLoop
1137			}
1138		}
1139	
1140		require.NoError(t, beadCreateErr)
1141		require.ErrorIs(t, wr.err, context.Canceled)
1142		require.NotNil(t, wr.result)
1143		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&activeEvents), "watch mode must still emit loop.active progress")
1144	
1145		out := logBuf.String()
1146		transition := "taking next ready bead from queue: ddx-ready-after-idle — spec: define full DDx temp cleanup in work cycle"
1147		header := "▶ ddx-ready-after-idle: spec: define full DDx temp cleanup in work cycle"
1148		assert.Contains(t, out, "\n12:34:56 "+transition+"\n")
1149		assert.Contains(t, out, header)
1150		assert.Less(t, strings.Index(out, transition), strings.Index(out, header))
1151	}

1203	// TestLoop_OnceFlagStillExits covers ddx-dc157075 back-compat: --once must
1204	// still terminate the loop after exactly one ready bead is processed, even
1205	// when more beads are queue-ready. This guards against an over-eager fix
1206	// that drops the Once gate.
1207	func TestLoop_OnceFlagStillExits(t *testing.T) {
1208		store, _, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
1209	
1210		var executed []string
1211		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
1212			Store: store,
1213			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
1214				executed = append(executed, beadID)
1215				return ExecuteBeadReport{
1216					BeadID:    beadID,
1217					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
1218					SessionID: "sess-once",
1219					ResultRev: "cafebabe",
1220				}, nil
1221			}),
1222		}
1223	
1224		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
1225		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
1226	
1227		// Even in watch-capable runtimes, once mode must still cause the loop to
1228		// exit after one bead.
1229		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
1230			Mode: executeloop.ModeOnce,
1231		})
1232		require.NoError(t, err)
1233		require.NotNil(t, result)
1234	
1235		assert.Len(t, executed, 1, "--once must process exactly one bead")
1236		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Attempts)
1237		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Successes)
1238	}

1240	// TestLoop_ExplicitDrainExits covers drain-and-exit semantics: an empty queue
1241	// is terminal unless watch mode is selected.
1242	func TestLoop_ExplicitDrainExits(t *testing.T) {
1243		store := bead.NewStore(t.TempDir())
1244		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
1245	
1246		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
1247			Store: store,
1248			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
1249				t.Fatalf("executor must not run when queue is empty")
1250				return ExecuteBeadReport{}, nil
1251			}),
1252		}
1253	
1254		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
1255		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
1256	
1257		// Drain mode exits when the queue is empty rather than idling. A
1258		// bounded-time context guards against a regression that would hang.
1259		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 2*time.Second)
1260		defer cancel()
1261	
1262		start := time.Now()
1263		result, err := worker.Run(ctx, rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
1264			Mode: executeloop.ModeDrain,
1265		})
1266		elapsed := time.Since(start)
1267	
1268		require.NoError(t, err, "drain mode must return cleanly without timing out")
1269		require.NotNil(t, result)
1270		assert.True(t, elapsed < time.Second,
1271			"explicit poll=0 must exit promptly when queue is empty; took %s", elapsed)
1272		assert.True(t, result.NoReadyWork)
1273		assert.Equal(t, 0, result.Attempts)
1274	}
